TextENGNew: Early the next morning. Une and the others were in the school courtyard that had been designated over the radio.
Roughly thirty of the Sonozaki family's toughest men surrounded them, scanning every direction with vigilant eyes.
changeReason: Old says "Sonozaki Group's toughest men" (implying yakuza organization); new says "Sonozaki family's toughest men" -- different organizational characterization.

TextENGNew: A helicopter was approaching. Une was the only one being extracted.
``Take care! Consider our debt repaid for saving us!``
``I hope the secret in your body can help save everyone.``
``...Thank you for looking after me...``
Une was dressed in airtight protective clothing. All of the JSDF personnel aboard the helicopter wore the same.
changeReason: Old says Une was "forced to wear airtight clothes" and "JSDF men"; new says "dressed in airtight protective clothing" and "JSDF personnel" -- "forced" implies coercion not present in Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``It is a bit disappointing that we're being left behind, though.``
``I'm sorry. Until we fully understand the cause of this illness, we can't permit anyone to leave the city.``
changeReason: Old includes the η17 code name reference that Tomitake catches himself saying; new omits this detail entirely, losing the self-correction moment.

TextENGNew: ``So even though Une-chan's situation is resolved, we're still stuck here in survival mode for now.``
``Well, you'll be safe as long as you stay here. I expect this strange illness will die down with time.``
``The number of crazed people is already declining. Peace will return to this city before long.``
The rioters who had surrendered their hearts to demons continued killing each other without rest.
But as Kasai said, they would eventually wipe themselves out, and the chaos would subside.
According to what Tomitake heard over the radio, preparations were already underway to send relief to Shishibone City and the surrounding area.
changeReason: Old specifies "food, water, and other necessities would be transported by air"; new generalizes to just "preparations were underway to send relief" and adds "surrounding area".

TextENGNew: ...An upheaval of this magnitude couldn't be resolved so easily.
But there was a light of hope. Une was the key.
``...Thank you...``
``Do your best out there. I'm glad to welcome you to this planet.``
``...I'm so happy. ...I'm super happy. Kfufufufufufufufu...``
Une waved from the helicopter as it lifted away.
The club members all waved innocently back at her.
Only Rika watched her departure with an absent expression.
There was something in Une's parting smile that she couldn't quite put into words...
Afterward, she was transferred to Tokyo's National Institute of Infectious Diseases.
With the disaster already affecting hundreds of thousands of people, a cover-up was no longer feasible. The truth was disclosed to the world.
The world erupted in panic over this mysterious virus capable of driving humans randomly violent.
Of course, the classified research institutions of the major powers -- those already aware of neuroparasites -- were far more alarmed.
Samples of Une's cerebrospinal fluid were collected and rushed to the most secure research facilities around the globe.
``...My seeds have reached every corner of this blue flower bed. ...Kfufufufu, I'm so happy. ...I'm super happy...``
``Foolish leader of the horned people... Tremble in fear at the sin you have committed by unleashing that terrifying one upon our world...``
``It's already too late. Your and Takano's evil scheme is finished. Auauau!``
``... ... ...Tamurahime-no-Mikoto. If you have something to say, why not say it clearly for once?``
``There's nothing worth asking. She was bullying Une and trying to spread Hinamizawa Syndrome everywhere -- she's in no position to accuse anyone.``
``That doesn't make sense, though. ...Hanyuu, the Syndrome is a trait of the tainted people to her, right? So why would she try to spread it? She'd be contaminating her own land.``
``Tomitake said Takano was going to defeat a 'devil' and save humanity. Then she started spreading eta-173 to stop that devil.``
``...She's both trying to save humanity and trying to destroy it. Those goals contradict each other. Unless the devil is something far worse than eta-173...``
``A mere child of man, let alone the priestess of the foolish horned people, could never comprehend my actions, even if I explained them a thousand times.``
``Please don't underestimate me. Unlike her, my head isn't stuffed with cream puffs.``
``Rika, I have absolutely no idea what you're getting at.``
``Tamura loathes the horned people, so why would she try to spread them beyond Hinamizawa? ...Think about it. The only reason she would is if there were something even worse to stop.``
The horned people and Une's people had equally strong roots.
On top of that, Tamura had called Une a devil.
And she told Takano she would save humanity...
``...You had Takano spread eta-173... so that Une couldn't spread herself...?``
``Yes -- just as those infected by Une couldn't be infected by eta-173, the reverse was also true. You used the lesser evil to block the greater one.``
``But if she infects them with us, the weak-minded will go berserk! There's no way Tamura would accept such a harmful solution!``
``How do you know that? You only convinced yourself of that after communicating with Une. How do you know she wasn't feeding you false information?``
``I see. ...It appears the leader's foolishness has not infected her priestess.``
``That means you made a desperate choice. ...You decided that handing over some of your land to the horned people was preferable to losing everything to Une.``
``Isn't that right...?! ...Which means... you knew about Une all along, didn't you...?``
``...Hers is an accursed race that brought great catastrophe upon their mother world...``
``Kfufufufufufufufu...!! This is super fun! Kfufufufufufufufu!``
``Une...! It's been a while.``
``Foolish Hanyuu. Behold her true nature, and tremble at the sin you have committed!``
``Tamurahime-no-Mikoto... You totally got the jump on me. The moment I woke up and tried to bloom, she planted me in a garden of Hanyuu's roots!``
``I'm sure she did. This cream-puff-brained idiot is a useless weed, but her roots are strong. You couldn't overwhelm her, so you waited... for us to bring you to uninfected ground.``

``...You tried to unleash your infection all at once, but since Takano had already spread eta-173, there was nowhere for you to take root. So you planted yourself among us, playing the victim, biding your time until we took you outside the infected zone...``
``I was at a total loss. But then a gullible fool who bought my story just happened to show up. ...Kfufufufufufufufu.``
...Even though her brain was an empty cream puff, lacking even the cream, Hanyuu finally realized the truth.
She had believed that she and Une understood each other completely after their telepathic exchange.
Hanyuu had naively shared everything about herself because she was trusting by nature. But Une had not.
...She had cleverly concealed her true nature and her terrifying agenda in order to deceive Hanyuu.
``Which means... Tamurahime-no-Mikoto. You saw through her from the beginning, didn't you?``
``Know that the schemes of such an accursed race could never deceive my insight.``
Tamura had known all along what Une was really after.
Une's true goal... was to propagate herself throughout every corner of this blue planet's vast flower bed, and consume it entirely.
``If she succeeded, all of you would perish. So you had to fight. ...Just as you once fought against the horned people.``
Hanyuu claimed Tamura had been cruel to her, but it probably wasn't mere cruelty. From Tamura's perspective, she had been protecting the Earth's flower bed from invaders.
Fortunately, it was discovered that Hanyuu's people couldn't survive outside Hinamizawa, so they managed to reach a coexistence agreement.
But if a new species arrived -- one not confined to such a small territory...
``I see. To you, she truly is a devil. ...And so you used Takano to stop her.``
``...But calling her a devil that would destroy humanity is an exaggeration. She might be a devil to your people, but that's a different matter from humanity's destruction.``
``...I see. That appears to be the limit of the horned people's priestess's understanding. Foolish.``
``Kfufufufufufufufu... It's super too late now, you idiots! We're totally consuming this whole planet!``
``Now, awaken, my children! My seeds...!! Sink your roots into this world and bloom, bloom, bloom!!``
Une spread her arms as though unfurling wings... and issued commands to all of her seeds scattered across the globe.
The children of humanity couldn't quarantine Une's seeds with their mere concept of disease prevention.
The researchers studying her across the world were already infected...
America. The state of Georgia, Atlanta.
America's Centers for Disease Control and Prevention was the fortress on the front lines of humanity's defense against disease.
Yet in a classified ward, the neuroparasite research division had been studying her children.
They had taken every precaution and used the most advanced equipment... but their techniques were woefully inadequate against an organism that transcended human understanding.
The head researcher was the first to collapse, on a bus during his day off.
People rushed to help him one after another, and they collapsed as well, until the driver himself lost consciousness.
Fortunately the bus had already stopped. ...But that was no silver lining.
Because neither humanity nor any inhuman being possessed the means to halt the spread of Une's children.
The same thing happened in Sverdlovsk Oblast in the Russian Federation, right in Yekaterinburg's research district.
Of course, it happened in Japan's National Institute of Infectious Diseases in Shinjuku, Tokyo, as well.
And from there, they spread through the transportation networks that humanity had so recklessly overdeveloped.
Humanity, however, had no means of detecting it.
Roughly two weeks after the initial infection, when Une's children had proliferated throughout the entire planet...
The infected -- though at this point, 'the infected' and 'all of humanity' were essentially synonymous -- succumbed to the first symptom.
People were struck by overwhelming drowsiness... and one after another, they crumpled and fell into a deep sleep.
They had become seed beds.
Once they had consumed all the nutrients from their hosts, they burst forth from the hosts' bodies, beautiful and terrible, like flowers blooming.
Because Une's people had no intention of coexisting with humanity whatsoever.
Once they had consumed their flower bed, they would return to drifting through space in search of the next.
Seven weeks after the initial infection, humanity's population had been reduced to twenty percent of its former size.
Of course, even that twenty percent was being steadily culled.
And then they would return to space once more, seeking another organism with a brain to serve as their host.
Afterward, only the hosts of the horned people -- those who couldn't be infected by that accursed species -- would remain.
Which meant that only those in this region whom Takano had infected with eta-173, and who hadn't turned violent, would survive.
Far too few humans remained to sustain civilized society.
And so, eventually, they too would perish completely...
``Kfufufufufufufu! Khahahahahahahaha...!!``
``Super fun! Super happy! Super awesome! Super super duper! Super kfufuhahahahahaha!!``
``Consuming everything is my people's only entertainment. And consuming an entire planet is so, so rare. This is super the best!``

``Once my people have consumed them all, no one will be able to stop us. When faced with me, even you horned people with your equal strength are powerless, since we're immune to each other. Right, Hanyuu?``
``...You really pulled one over on us. You played weak and used us to advance your plans. Well done.``
``I couldn't fool Tamurahime-no-Mikoto. ...She was clever.``
``Do not underestimate me. Know that I am the leader of the people ruling over this nation of Yamato!``
``But that horn-headed fool and her priestess! You two were super easy to trick! Kfufufufufufufu! When the god's an idiot, her priestess is an idiot too!``
``When the god's incompetent, so is the priestess! You're a useless god with nothing useful to your name, Hanyuu! You're even a bigger fool than I imagined! Kfufufufufufufu!``
``...What is this? She irritates me five times more than Tamura.``
``I completely agree... It really is maddening to be called incompetent...``
``Know that you are indeed incompetent! You are tainted people, and now foolish people! You should have heeded my warnings!``
``Well, I didn't know Une was going to destroy the world! It's not my fault!``
``You know that all other races are enemies!! Yet you handed everything over to her! There is a limit to stupidity!!``
``Auauau!! You could have just told us that, Tamura! So it's your fault, Tamura!``
``Be quiet! I will not listen to you tainted people!!``
``Kfufufufuhahahahahahahaha! This is super fun! Khahahahahaha!!``
``Perish, you stupid gods of this blue flower bed! Your stupid humans, your incompetent priestesses -- consume them all! Consume, consume, consume! Kfufuhahahahaha!!``
Une cackled with delight while Hanyuu and Tamura hurled insults at each other like children.
...It was quite pathetic that this was a quarrel among gods who had brought about the extinction of humanity.
Rika pressed a hand to her forehead as though nursing a headache, but her frustration was winning out over her composure.
``Listen. I don't particularly consider myself a human, and I don't care if our world is destroyed. But there's one thing I cannot overlook.``
``...The fact that you looked at me, Rika Furude, who has survived hundreds of deaths, and thought, 'This girl is dumb enough to use' -- that is unforgivable.``
``...The word 'incompetent' is what you use for a man who goes off to a hot spring at a critical moment. Not for Rika Furude.``
``You intend to oppose me? You, a mere child of man? That's super funny. Kfufufufufufu!``
Rika pulled something from seemingly nowhere -- something that glinted with a cold, silver light.
``What is that...?``
``...Don't you recognize it? It's a kitchen knife. A cooking tool. The perfect instrument for chopping up ingredients.``
``Kfufufufufu! I'm super laughing right now! How do you plan to fight me with a tool of mankind? It can't reach me -- you know that!``
From the perspective of humans, these inhuman beings were like a reflection of the moon on water.
She could swing a weapon all she wanted in the world of man, but it wouldn't even ripple the water's surface.
However, for them to manifest in the world of man, they required a priestess. In human terms, a queen carrier. When they were in their queen carrier, they were as vulnerable as any human.
If she could defeat Une's queen carrier -- was that the right term? Or was it better to call it 'Une herself'? -- then it would be like cutting down a weed at the root.
``However, my body is super beyond your reach. You can't even leave Shishibone City, so you can't lay a hand on me! Super too bad!``
``Nearly all of my people have been taken captive... I have no idea where she is at present...``
``That's enough! Honestly, it's your fault for being so petty, Tamura, and holding a thousand-year grudge! And it's your fault for not warning us, just because of your stubborn pride!``
``I will never surrender a single grain of rice that has been dedicated to me!!``
``I was starving!! You could have spared a little!!``
``...Stop it, you two. Besides, this battle isn't over yet.``
``Oh? And how are you going to face me all by yourself when I've super enveloped your entire world? That's super funny!``
``You're right. This battle is already over. As far as you two can conceive. But from my perspective... it hasn't even begun.``
``...No, perhaps it's more accurate to say this battle was over the moment you revealed your true nature to me.``
``What nonsense is your priestess spouting? How can she possibly save the world in this situation?!``
``Khihihihihi! I want to know! I super want to know!``
``I'm the one who wants to know! It's too late! The world is ending! How are we supposed to save it now?!``
``...Hey. How can you not understand? Have you forgotten how this is used?``
``Au? You're going to hack open Une's head with the knife?``
``If she were not a god, I would command you to behead Une this instant!``
``You two both have your heads stuffed with cream-free cream puffs. I'll show you how this knife is actually used.``
``Oh? And what do you totally think you're doing?``
``The cause of your defeat was revealing your true identity and whereabouts to me.``
``Huh?! You know where Une is right now?! How do you know when even Tamura doesn't?!``
``S-Still, even if you did know, there's nothing you can do if she's not right here!``
``It doesn't matter where she is right now. Back then, she was here in Okinomiya. That's all I need to know.``
``What are you saying? I super don't get it.``
``Let me tell you something. You're going to lose...``
``Because you made me angry.``
Let us go back...
Back to the moment Une became aware of the other Une and awakened her on this planet.
It was the day before Takano began spreading eta-173.
Since Une had only just awakened, her presence was still faint. Tamura had not yet pinpointed her location.
Une had assessed how weak Tamura's roots were and was preparing to bloom all at once, to swallow the entire flower bed in a single stroke.
Tamura had given up on locating Une directly and instead welcomed Takano as her priestess, preparing a preemptive strike.
Her plan: have Takano spread eta-173 before Une could act.
Which meant that at this moment in time, neither Une nor Takano had spread anything at all. The world had yet to change.
In other words, if Une's ambition could be stopped on this day, everything would be prevented before it started.
But that was impossible. Without a time machine!
``...Khihihi! I've super woken up. ...I couldn't fool Tamurahime-no-Mikoto, but that doesn't matter. I just need to prepare before she can make her move.``
Une's ambition to conquer the world with her kind was born right here, in this small town of Okinomiya.
No one in the world had noticed this terrifying resolve. However.
``Good afternoon. Are you Une-chan? Are you...?``
``...Who are you...?``
``Rena, did you find her?! Oh, this must be her!``
``Yeah, it is, it is! She looks exactly like the portrait!``
``Rika is quite good at drawing, isn't she? I'm rather impressed!``
The older girl with a ponytail held up a sketchbook with a portrait that bore an exact likeness to me.
I was suddenly surrounded and couldn't understand why.
``...I finally found you, Une.``
``Wh-Who are you...?``
``Normally, our first meeting would be weeks from now. But I only met you just the other day, in a different timeline where you showed me your true, ugly face.``
``Fufufu! You shouldn't underestimate me and Rika, Une!! We've already seen your scheme to conquer the world! And Rika slashed it to pieces with her kitchen knife!``
``...H-How did you see through my plan?!``
``See? The leader of the horned people and her priestess aren't so powerless or incompetent after all. Now apologize to me.``
``Flash! This is the ancient god-binding treasure normally kept sealed in the Furude Shrine's ritual storehouse!``
``Now, everyone! Bind her up with this sacred rope! Nipah~♪``
``...Y-You're tying me up! Ahhhh!!``
``Ohh! To think you found Une so readily when I could not! You have my gratitude, children of man!``
``You've shown yourself, Tamurahime-no-Mikoto!``
``By the way, you're equally at fault. If you had just set aside your pride and asked us for help, you wouldn't have needed Takano as a priestess at all.``
``I am the proud Tamurahime-no-Mikoto! Know that I would never lower myself to beg the savage and tainted horned people for assistance!``
``That's exactly why you're equally guilty.@k@|@y@vS25/05/NRIK_1461. Flash!!``
``W-What?!! The god-binding treasure?!! Ahhh!!``
``Now we've captured all the instigators of this mess! Au!``
``This tragedy could have been prevented entirely if you and Tamura weren't on such terrible terms.``
``It's not my fault! Tamura was the stingy one! She had mountains of offerings and I was just a little hungry! It was practically theft of services, not actual theft!``
``In other words, you stole from her. So you're just as guilty!@k@|@y@vS25/05/NRIK_1481. Flash!!!``
``Is that why you brought three sacred ropes?! Ahhh!!!``
